name: The Museum of Alchemists and Magicians of Old Prague
description: The history of occult science is turned into a creepshow at this lurid Prague tourist attraction.

name: Strahov Monastery
description: Two stunning 17th-century libraries and a cabinet of curiosities.

name: ‘Idiom’
description: A Jenga-style cylindrical tower of books that appears to go on forever.

name: Speculum Alchemiae
description: The hidden lair of an alchemist rediscovered in a 2002 flood.

name: Dripstone Wall
description: Countless grotesque faces stare out from this eerie Prague art wall.

name: Prague Astronomical Clock
description: Macabre astrological automaton clock dating to the late 1400s.

name: Olšany Cemetery
description: Walk through Czech history in Prague's largest graveyard.

name: Sedlec Ossuary "Bone Church"
description: A church of bones, decorated with 40,000 human skeletons.

name: Cross Club
description: A steampunk-styled three-floor factory churning out art, activism, and electronic beats.

name: Grotta
description: This artificial cave looks like something out of a fairytale.

name: Old Jewish Cemetery
description: Around 100,000 bodies are buried below the medieval headstones, many of which mark graves with multiple bodies stacked up to 12 deep.

name: Brown-Nosers
description: The risqué mixed-media sculptures are supposedly a metaphor for Czech politics.

name: Sex Machines Museum
description: The first museum in the world devoted to sexual gadgetry.

name: Hlava Franze Kafky (Franz Kafka's Head)
description: A bust of Franz Kafka spins in pieces, reflecting the writer's inner torment.

name: Tančící dům - Dancing House of Prague
description: This tipsy office building in downtown Prague was originally called “Fred and Ginger.”

name: Paternoster Lift
description: This doorless 20th-century elevator doesn't stop or slow down.

name: Man Hanging Out
description: What looks like a suicide in progress is actually a statue of Sigmund Freud.

name: Nuclear Bunker Museum
description: Buried five stories underground, this Soviet bunker is packed with gas masks and Cold War paraphernalia.

name: Museum of Communism
description: This Prague museum collects brutalist relics and memories from the country's former Soviet rule.

name: Hrdlička Museum of Anthropology
description: A collection of anatomical samples from one of the first anthropologists to suggest that humans reached North America by crossing the Bering Strait.

name: R2-D2 of Prague
description: Street artists transformed an abandoned air vent into the beloved Star Wars robot.

name: The Deer Moat Pedestrian Tunnel
description: The tunnel's elegant proportions keep you snug with no claustrophobia freak-out.

name: The Thief's Arm at Kostel Sv. Jakuba Vetsiho
description: A 400-year-old mummified arm dangles from the ceiling of St. James the Greater, serving as a warning to thieves.

name: Franz Kafka Museum
description: An eerie, existential museum dedicated to the author of "The Metamorphosis."

name: Lennon Wall
description: Despite attempts by local police to discourage it,  Beatles-inspired graffiti has been a means for artists to air grievances for decades.

name: Statue of King Wenceslas Riding an Upside-Down Dead Horse
description: A mocking tribute to the past and modern leadership of Prague.

name: ‘Proudy’
description: This automated sculpture of two men peeing will spell out text messages with their micturition.

name: Gulliver Airship
description: What looks like a crashed Zeppelin is actually a bright, airy reading room perched atop a modern museum.

name: Vinárna Čertovka
description: This walkway is so narrow it needs its own light to stop pedestrian traffic jams.

name: Podobenství s Lebkou (Parable With Skull)
description: An existential riddle in the world's largest ancient castle.

name: 'Trifot'
description: An Orwellian sculpture with eyeball cameras watches passersby.

name: The Penguins at Kampa Park
description: A series of playful yellow penguins share a serious message about the environment.

name: Old New Synagogue
description: Synagogue built from the stones of Solomon's temple contains the golem of Prague.

name: Museum of Miniatures
description: Microscopic scenes; some call it novelty, some call it art.

name: St. Valentine’s Shoulder Blade
description: A saintly relic hidden in a Prague church basement.

name: 'Il Commendatore' ('Cloak of Conscience')
description: This spooky statue sits outside a famous Prague theater.

name: Melník Chapel of Bones
description: Underneath this small church are 15,000 bones, arranged by an obsessive academic.

name: Municipal House
description: A turn of the century building boasting some of the most beautiful art nouveau architecture in Prague.

name: Defenestration of Prague Window
description: The palace window through which Protestant nobles flung two Catholic regents, thus sparking the Thirty Years' War.

name: Kostel Svatého Jiří Ghost Church
description: Thirty creepy ghosts now inhabit this decaying 14th-century church.

name: Brno Ossuary
description: Europe's second largest ossuary wasn't discovered until 2001.

name: The Devil Heads
description: Two monstrous faces loom over a village in Czechia.

name: Žižkov Tower
description: Looming communist pillar with a Czech twist.

name: Pravčická brána (Pravčice Gate)
description: Europe's largest natural bridge has a 19th century royal chateau built into its side.

name: Quo Vadis
description: A sculpture of the iconic East German car on four legs is a tribute to those who traveled to Prague seeking asylum.

name: Vojan Gardens
description: The oldest garden in Prague, once part of an medieval orchard.

name: Statue of St. Wilgefortis
description: A crucified and bearded female saint.

name: Spiral Tower of Krásno
description: A spiral staircase twists to the top of this unusual Czech lookout tower.

name: Memorial to the Victims of Communism
description: Increasingly decayed figures stand as a powerful reminder of the human costs of Czech Communism.

name: London Booster
description: A bus with a butt and biceps to put Olympic athletes to shame.

name: Prague Meridian
description: The slender brass strip in Prague's Old Town Square was used to tell time from 1652 to 1918.

name: Velka Amerika
description: This former quarry is now a picturesque gorge that hides a sunken lake.

name: Muzeum Alchymie (Alchemy Museum)
description: A museum of alchemy and a basement laboratory all housed in an ancient building in a Gothic tower.

name: The Heydrich Terror Memorial
description: Bullet holes in the church wall are a reminder of the violence that followed the assassination of a high-ranking Nazi officer.

name: Prague Pneumatic Post
description: The last municipal pneumatic post system in the world was in operation for over 100 years, and may run again.

name: Jan Palach Memorial
description: Monument to a suicide protesting the Soviet invasion.

name: Český Ráj (Bohemian Paradise)
description: Bohemian paradise covered in majestic forests.

name: Statue of Franz Kafka
description: A surreal statue honoring Prague's favorite literary son.

name: Periodic Table of Charles University
description: A giant interactive periodic table with real samples of the elements.

name: The Czech's Capuchin Crypt
description: Mummified monks and the accidentally interred, in a 17th-century crypt.

name: David Černý's Embryo
description: One of the famous Czech artist's lesser-known Prague installations appears to be a glowing fetus affixed to a drainpipe.

name: Prague Metronome
description: Steadily reminding Czechia of its past.

name: Adršpach-Teplice Rocks
description: Enormous sandstone towers loom within a Czech forest.

name: Dolní Morava Sky Walk
description: The world's greatest slide descends 18 stories from this looping mountaintop viewing platform.

name: Squat Milada
description: A former squatter's paradise outside Prague.

name: Macocha Abyss
description: The deepest sinkhole in Central Europe comes with a grisly tale and an unusual nickname: “The Stepmother Abyss.”

name: Rozhledna Doubravka
description: A massive, abstract lookout tower providing a panoramic view of Prague.

name: Houska Castle
description: Folklore says this medieval fortress was plopped atop a portal to hell to trap the demons below.

name: 'Babies'
description: Straight out of a nightmare, barcode-faced babies climb up and down Prague's television tower.

name: Palladium Shopping Mall
description: Throughout this shopping mall are medieval ruins that were discovered during the mall's construction.

name: Memento Mori: 'Of One's Own Volition'
description: A controversial artwork draws attention to the dark history of Prague's "Suicide Bridge."

name: Klatovy Catacomb Mummies
description: These 17th-century mummies are no longer naturally preserved, but remain on display today.

name: 'Llamas (He and She)'
description: The rusted creatures wait to be discovered in Prague's botanical garden.

name: Museum of the Infant Jesus
description: This Czech church doubles as something of a permanent fashion exhibition for a statue of the Infant Jesus.

name: Křtiny Ossuary
description: Mysterious painted skulls in Moravia.

name: Hotel Ještěd
description: This unusually shaped mountaintop tower looks like a UFO floating over the city.

name: Velvet Revolution Monument
description: Nine bronze hands protruding from a wall honor the student uprising that led to the fall of Czechoslovakia's Communist government.

name: Museum of Historical Chamber Pots and Toilets
description: Dedicated to the unsung history of human waste.

name: Faustův Dům (Faust House)
description: A Baroque mansion with ties to legendary magicians and alchemists.

name: Putim Ossuary
description: The mystery of the Putim Ossuary involves a tricorn hat.

name: Pilsen Historical Underground
description: Underneath the city that invented pilsners are a series of historic tunnels that have been storing beer for centuries.

name: Znojmo Catacombs
description: This network of conjoined cellars was riddled with traps and supposedly allowed the townspeople to pretend their homes were deserted.

name: Kabourek
description: A grumpy old water goblin guarding an old mill on the Devil’s Stream in Prague.

name: Location of Saint John of Nepomuk's Martyrdom
description: A plaque marks the spot where the saint was shoved off the Charles Bridge and into the river.

name: The Indecent Little Man on the Church of St. James
description: A cheeky architectural easter egg.

name: The Lidice Children
description: The soulless eyes of 82 bronze children stare on forever in remembrance of a horrifying World War II massacre.

name: Černy's Buried Soviet Tank
description: A half-buried vehicle in Prague's is the latest iteration in a long-running piece by Czech artist David Černý.

name: Nuclear Shelter 10-Z
description: A former top secret nuclear fallout shelter now operates like a hotel stuck in the Cold War.

name: Karel Hynek Mácha Statue
description: Tucked away in a Prague park, a statue beloved by romantics honors a poet so nice they buried him twice.

name: Panská Skála
description: Tall columns of volcanic basalt make up this improbable-seeming rock formation.

name: The Unfinished Gothic Church of Panenský Týnec
description: The 14th-century ruins are said to contain healing powers.

name: House of the Black Madonna
description: A significant example of Czech cubist architecture—a style you won't find anywhere else in the world.

name: The Bear Moat at Český Krumlov Castle
description: Be(w)ar(e) the moat around this historic Czech castle.

name: Lower Vítkovice
description: A massive 1830s industrial site once known as Czechia's "Iron Heart" has been transformed into a playground of science, technology, and art.

name: The Old Wastewater Treatment Plant Stará čistírna
description: One of the oldest, well-preserved water treatment facilities in all of Europe.

name: Prague Hlavni Nadrazi Vestibule
description: A historic and beautiful Art Nouveau chamber in a historic railway station.

name: Saint Barbara's Church
description: An enormous and lavish Gothic church in Czechia.

name: Bradáč
description: The medieval "Bearded Man" relief still serves as a clever flood warning marker.

name: Josef Vachal Museum
description: Unique house covered in a nightmarish mural of demons, ghosts and monsters.

name: Pekelné Doly (Mines of Hell)
description: The largest manmade caves in Europe now function as a motorbike club's headquarters.

name: Winton Children Farewell Memorial
description: Dedicated to the man who saved hundreds of children from Nazi-occupied Czechoslovakia.

name: 'Lilith' by David Černý
description: A colossal statue of a woman hugging a modern building.

name: Hunger Wall of Prague
description: Local folklore claims that this wall was not built for defense, but to provide labor during a time of famine.

name: Kaiserbad Spa
description: Before serving as James Bond's Casino Royale, this Victorian-era spa catered to the world's elite.

name: Karel Zeman Museum
description: Dedicated to the works of the legendary special effects artist.

name: Entropa
description: Massive installation of Europe's negative stereotypes.

name: J.A. Comenius Museum
description: Dedicated to the "father of modern education," this museum documents the history of education in Czechia.

name: Kristek's House
description: Of all the houses that grace this neighborhood, only one is a testament to surreal architecture.

name: Brno Astronomical Clock
description: A peculiar clock commemorates a historic victory won by ringing the noonday bell an hour early.

name: Libušín a Maměnka
description: A folk-art cottage and hotel straight from a Slavic fairy tale.

name: The State Chateau of Hluboká
description: Heralded as one of the prettiest castles in all of Czechia.

name: Skalní obydlí Lhotka (Cliff Dwelling in Lhotka)
description: Every part of this unusual home is built into the sandstone rock.

name: Holy Trinity Column
description: An ornate plague column – built to celebrate life – claimed the lives of numerous artisans during its construction.

name: Emmaus Monastery
description: A quasi-futuristic roof sits atop a medieval Bohemian monastery damaged during World War II.

name: Cave of the Blanik Knights
description: An army of sleeping knights sculpted by a self-taught local artist beautifully depict an old Czech legend.

name: Brno Dragon
description: This hanging crocodile is thought to be the "dragon" that once plagued a Czech city.

name: Military Technical Museum of Lesany
description: This Czech military museum is home to pink tank that was once a symbol of art and political rebellion.

name: Hunger Stones of Děčín
description: When the river dries up, these stones start talking.

name: Vrbice Wine Cellars
description: A mesmerizing landscape of 18th-century cellars carved out of the land.

name: Andělská Hora Castle
description: This ruined castle perched atop a rocky peak is a great place for a hike.

name: Pilgrimage Church of Saint John of Nepomuk
description: The sanctuary holds what was once believed to be the saint's incorruptible tongue.

name: Hotel Radium Palace
description: Visitors flock to the historic birthplace of radiobalneology for its therapeutic radium treatments.

name: Poledník Lookout Tower
description: A Cold War secret surveillance tower used to spy on radio communications.

name: Rotonde Saint-Martin (St Martin’s Rotunda)
description: The largest and best-preserved rotunda in Prague.

name: Velké Losiny Château
description: This renaissance château was home to an infamous witch hunter who sent a hundred suspected witches to their deaths.

name: Olomouc's Communist Clock
description: Tiny communists hammer away on the Olomouc Astronomical Clock.

name: Tiské Stěny
description: Towering sandstone rock formations where 'The Chronicles of Narnia' was filmed.

name: Hostina Obrů (Feast of Giants)
description: An unusual bronze bus shelter is actually an art installation commenting on the city's history with the Nazi party.

name: Iron Knight
description: The statue of a cursed knight is said to come to life every 100 years.

name: The Samurai of Brno
description: A peculiar samurai statue overlooks the center of Brno.

name: 'Slovanská epopej' ('Slav Epic')
description: Art Nouveau painter Alphonse Mucha spent 18 years creating these monumental paintings of Slavic history and mythology.

name: Theresienstadt (Terezín Concentration Camp)
description: Created as a "model concentration camp" to hide Nazi atrocities from the international community, this former camp-ghetto now holds a museum and memorial honoring the lives lost within.

name: Karl Marx Monument
description: A larger-than-life statue of the German revolutionist stands proud in a small town where he never lived.

name: Pohádková Zahrádka (Fairytale Garden)
description: Before building her own tombstone, an untrained artist created a concrete menagerie in her garden.

name: 'The Thief'
description: A cheeky reminder about the value of art dangles above one of Olomouc's busiest streets.

name: Vanocni dum
description: A mansion where wonders of Christmas are celebrated all year long.

name: Stray Boulder (Bludný Kámen)
description: Locals say the stone that marks the spot where the gallows once stood can lead you astray.

name: 11-Year-Old Mozart Statue
description: This strange statue depicts a young boy's body with an adult Mozart's head and a single wing.

name: Battle of Prague Monument
description: A small monument on the outskirts of Prague, where one of the bloodiest conflicts in the country's history was decided.

name: Muzeum Samorostů (Museum of Driftwood)
description: 600 stumps, branches, and trunks of different shapes and sizes.

name: Orloj Martina Chaloupky
description: Abandoned substation turned village treasure.

name: Miniature Park Boheminium
description: Stroll past dozens of intricately-detailed scale replicas of buildings from all over Czechia.

name: Museum of Romani Culture
description: In this Romani neighborhood lies a museum dedicated to the culture and history of the Roma.

name: Moved Church of Most
description: This Gothic church was wheeled in its entirety to a new location a half a mile away.

name: Church of Blessed Maria Restituta Kafka
description: A striking, modern church devoted to a nun who loved God and beer.

name: Bítov Castle
description: The castle in the romantic landscape of Podyjí houses what may be Europe's largest collection of taxidermy dogs.

name: Muzeum Betlémů Karlštejn (Museum of Nativity Scenes)
description: This quaint museum tells about more than just the birth of Jesus: "betlémy" are an important piece of Czech history.

name: Fort Stachelberg
description: This fortress was designed to prevent a Nazi invasion but was never used.

name: FairyTale House of Puppets and Wine
description: A museum of Czech puppetry and wine in an allegedly haunted 14th-century stone house.

name: Doubice Wooden Sculptures and Tanks
description: This village in Bohemian Switzerland hosts more than 300 wood carvings inspired by fairytales, folklore, and other oddities, as well as a series of Communist-era tanks

name: Harfenice Cave
description: Human figures and faces are carved into the sandstone surrounding this cave in Czechia.

name: Stork Nest Farm
description: Czech architects borrow from the nest design of the stork to revitalize a farmstead, hoping to inspire the same loyalty the birds had shown.

name: Národní památník odposlechu (National Interception Memorial)
description: This trio of reliefs—an ear, a mouth, and a pair of eyes—carved into the walls of flooded quarries is a monument to spying.

name: The Zwinger Gardens
description: Once home to artillery, this garden offers more peace today than it did during its war-torn past.

name: Zapa Concrete Holubice
description: A concrete plant shaped like four giant soldiers commemorates one of the most decisive battles of the Napoleonic Wars.

name: The Devil's Column
description: According to local legend, the devil shattered this column after losing a bet.

name: First Roma Stolpersteine
description: These small, brass stones commemorate the first Roma victims of the Holocaust.

name: Punk Monument
description: This pink tractor statue pays tribute to a legendary underground punk band.

name: Tribute to Věra
description: This moving sculpture depicts contortionist positions based on photographs of gymnast Věra Čáslavská, who protested the Soviet invasion of Czechoslovakia.

name: Kovozoo (Metal Zoo)
description: Hundreds of "animals" created from scrap metal, alongside a collection of other oddities.

name: Mausoleum of Yugoslav Soldiers
description: This almost a century old piece of architecture had been overlooked by most, except for the drunk and the homeless

name: Geigenbauer-Denkmal (Violin Maker Monument)
description: A monument to the small town of master violin makers and its countless luthiers.

name: Třeboň
description: In the town of Třeboň, carp is king.

name: TTTM Sapa (Little Hanoi)
description: Prague's "Little Hanoi" is one of the commercial centers of Czechia's Vietnamese community.

name: The Dragon of Trutnov
description: Each spring, residents of this Czech town hoist a 'slain' metal dragon onto the city hall to commemorate a local medieval legend

name: Tesla Stained-Glass Window
description: A beautiful window with the old Tesla Radio company logo.

name: 'Kladenská koule' ('Kladno Ball')
description: This spherical sculpture has also been dubbed "the steel orange" due to its likeness to a massive, sliced piece of citrus.

name: The Leaning Church of St. Peter of Alcantara
description: Once overlooking the town, this church now resides on a permanent tilt.

name: 'Hvězdicová Kotelna'
description: An unusual heating plant that has garnered notoriety for its distinct shape.

name: The VKOL Red Church
description: Instead of sermons, this church became home to millions of books.

name: Royal Mint Jáchymov Museum
description: Dedicated to the history of mining and minting in a region rich in silver and radium.

name: ‘Waiting Adam’
description: The smallest statue in Brno will keep you company under the clock on Česká Street.

name: Můstek Bridge
description: The ruins of a medieval bridge are visible inside a Prague Metro station.

name: Městská Elektrárna v Písku (Písek Electricity Museum)
description: The oldest hydroelectric plant in Czechia is now a museum.

name: House of Stone Maiden
description: A tragic tale of a young woman accused of witchcraft haunts this place.

name: Museum of Olomouc Cheese
description: This entire museum is dedicated to a single smelly delicacy.

name: Valley of the Mills (Údolí mlýnků)
description: A stream flows near the radioactive spa, where people place handmade miniatures of water mills and other strange devices.

name: Hrachovina
description: A metal forest of peas pays tribute to the founding father of genetics.

name: The Slavín Tomb at Vyšehrad Cemetery
description: This monumental neo-Renaissance tomb in Vyšehrad Cemetery is the final resting place for Czech cultural legends.

name: Abandoned Barrandov Pool
description: Czechoslovakia’s first competition pool, now a decaying ruin of a functionalist marvel.

name: Wallenstein's Death Room
description: The room where the famous commander of the Catholic forces was murdered by Irish assassins during the Thirty Years' War has been reconstructed with many of his original possessions.

name: Pomník Rudoarmějce
description: This statue of a Red Army Soldier has been moved and changed several times since its creation.

name: Abstrakce
description: This abstract sculpture once had a twin in Prague.

name: The Tunnels of Tabor
description: Hidden tunnels have sheltered the people of a Czech town for centuries.

name: Bunker of Civil Defence (Kryt Civilní Ochrany)
description: This former atomic bunker is now the Olomouc Institute of Remembrance, telling stories of the communist era.

name: Rock and Roll Garage
description: This colorful hotel is also a full-blown rock 'n' roll compound.

name: Dragon of Brezno
description: This massive, now-retired coal-mine excavator is said to resemble a mythical dragon.

name: ‘K-S 14’ Infantry Bunker
description: Witness to Nazi occupation, this bunker has managed to soldier on to this day.

name: Dlouhé Stráně Hydro Power Station
description: This power plant is at the edge of a Czech mountain.

name: Baťa’s Elevator Office
description: A Czech shoe magnate built his office inside a giant elevator.

name: Budweiser/Budvar Brewery
description: The original Budweiser has a long-standing naming dispute with its American counterpart.

name: Baroque Cemetery
description: A unique Baroque monument that creates a fascinating “theater of memory” focused on the themes of human transience and the hope for resurrection.

name: Bohnický Hřbitov Cemetery (Cemetery of Fools)
description: An old asylum graveyard that's been abandoned for half a century attracts those seeking a dark thrill.

name: KGB Muzeum
description: One man's personal collection of KGB memorabilia is presented along with his strange viewpoint.

name: Saint Michael's Church
description: This charming wooden church was disassembled and moved not once but twice before settling in this Prague garden.

name: Batalion Comic Book Museum and Club
description: This bar and museum are devoted to the controversial comic book work of Kája Saudek.

name: Aeroplane Bar Letka Tu-104
description: Crammed inside this grounded passenger plane is a bar that was once a Communist hang out and is now a ratty relic.
